summary.intent: Retrofit the reflect project to use qmd's agentic-specific CLI flags (--json, --files, --min-score, --full) so AI agents can consume structured output instead of plain text.
summary.outcome: Successfully integrated qmd's agentic interface across four files and pushed commit 9d724e8; also discovered and fixed a latent bug where search.py used --limit instead of qmd's correct -n flag.

repo: qmd ships its own Claude skill and MCP server; `qmd skill install --yes` creates a symlink at .claude/skills/qmd/ and should be called during `reflect init`
The keeper agent's evidence ladder should use qmd as the first rung (fastest) before descending to full session search or git history
qmd supports five query types: query, search, vsearch, get, multi-get, each with distinct agentic flags
qmd supports advanced query grammar prefixes: lex:, vec:, hyde:, intent: for when the hybrid default is insufficient

code.finding: Latent bug: code used --limit for qmd result count but qmd's correct flag is -n; also bumped query timeout to 60s to accommodate first-run llama.cpp compilation
reflect init now runs `qmd skill install --yes` to auto-install the qmd skill alongside reflect's own skill
Updated to document all agentic flags (--json, --files, --min-score, --all, --full, -n, --no-rerank) as first-class examples; now references qmd MCP server option
qmd promoted to first rung of evidence ladder; keeper now queries indexed wiki first before broader session/git search

workflow: qmd's --json flag produces structured ranked hits suitable for LLM consumption; --files returns only paths above a relevance floor; --full retrieves complete document content
llama.cpp is compiled from source on first qmd run, making the initial embed operation significantly slower than subsequent runs
Verifying import cleanliness after edits (checking for 18 flag mentions in skill) is a useful sanity check before committing

friction: qmd embed was blocked on first-run llama.cpp compilation from source, preventing completion of Test 1 during the session
The --limit vs -n flag discrepancy in search.py was a silent bug that would only manifest at runtime, not import time
open_items: Test 1 query still pending — waiting for background qmd embed task (bhwrn6d3j) to complete before retrying with corrected -n flag
Full 1-2-3 test sequence not yet validated end-to-end after the agentic flag changes
MCP server integration mentioned in skill docs but not yet wired into reflect's own tooling
